front: Work: a pilot crash-lands in the Sahara and meets the title character, a boy from Asteroid B-612
Work: adults mistake a drawing for a hat, but the title character recognizes it as a boa constrictor swallowing an elephant
Work: after three failed attempts to draw a sheep, the narrator draws a box with three air holes
Work: dedicated to Leon Werth 'when he was a little boy'
Work: a yellow-shaded figure falls 'softly as a tree falls'; reproduced without the figure as 'the loveliest and the saddest landscape in the world'
Author: disappeared over the Mediterranean; identity bracelet naming him and wife Consuelo found near the site
Author: worked for Aeropostale, detailed in Wind, Sand and Stars
Author: Sartre described this author as a precursor of 'literature of the machine'
Author: wrote Letter to a Hostage, addressing citizens of occupied France
